hypothesis: Systematic fine-tuning of an LLM on a curated dataset of physics-based simulation trajectories—where each trajectory encodes the full sequence of intermediate states, phase fractions, and temperatures produced by the JMA-kinetics simulator—will yield a domain-adapted model whose synthesis route success rate on multi-phase, non-adjacent challenges (analogous to Challenges 2 and 3) significantly exceeds that of the prompt-tuned GPT-4o baseline, because the model will internalize the nonlinear mapping between compositional/thermal control inputs and resulting phase fraction evolution that cannot be conveyed through few-shot prompting alone.
